Audience guide

Who PermitPulse Helps

PermitPulse turns public-record permit signals into first-pass property clarity for builders, agents, investors, and property operators before they buy, bid, build, list, quote, or promise a timeline.

View Sample Report Send One Address
ADU builders Contractors Agents Investors Expeditors

Public records already contain warning signs. City portals show records. PermitPulse turns those records into a plain-English risk snapshot.

No subscription. No dashboard. No monthly minimum. One address, one clear PDF.

ADU Builders

Check visible permit friction before you quote the backyard dream.

Pain points

  • unclear prior work
  • zoning or planning surprises
  • client expectations before design begins

How PermitPulse helps

  • checks available permit and planning records
  • flags manual confirmation needs
  • gives a plain-English next move
Check an ADU Property
Contractors and Remodelers

Know what the property record shows before you promise a timeline.

Pain points

  • client says the work was permitted, but records are unclear
  • scope may trigger permit questions
  • city lookup takes time during quoting

How PermitPulse helps

  • organizes visible public records
  • notes permit history clues
  • flags what needs confirmation before quoting
Check Before You Quote
Real Estate Agents

Give buyers and sellers a cleaner view of public-record risk before inspection drama.

Pain points

  • buyers ask about additions, ADUs, garage conversions, or remodels
  • sellers are not sure what records show
  • deal confidence drops when permit questions appear late

How PermitPulse helps

  • creates a shareable first-pass property record snapshot
  • separates found records from manual confirmation items
  • helps prepare better questions before escrow or inspection issues
Check a Listing
Investors

Check permit record friction before you underwrite the deal.

Pain points

  • unpermitted work risk
  • unclear ADU or conversion history
  • hidden timeline assumptions

How PermitPulse helps

  • checks available public records before deeper diligence
  • summarizes visible permit and planning clues
  • flags uncertainty before money moves
Check Before You Underwrite
Permit Expeditors

Use PermitPulse as a fast pre-screen before deeper city work.

Pain points

  • prospects send messy addresses with vague questions
  • early research eats time
  • some leads are not ready for full expediting

How PermitPulse helps

  • creates a clean first-pass research packet
  • organizes public-source screenshots and notes
  • helps decide whether deeper manual city work is worth it
Pre-Screen an Address
Start

Start with one address.

Send the property address and what you are trying to figure out. PermitPulse will prepare a first-pass public-record snapshot using available official sources where possible.

Order Permit Review Plus View Sample Report Text an Address

PermitPulse is a first-pass public-record review. It is not a legal opinion, architectural opinion, title report, survey, code determination, permit expeditor service, or permit approval guarantee.
